title: turpinia occidentalis subsp. occidentalis
intro: Turpinia occidentalis subsp. occidentalis, also known as the West Indian Turpinia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Passifloraceae, native to the West Indies. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Turpinia occidentalis subsp. occidentalis include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Turpinia occidentalis subsp. occidentalis is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, with the roots and leaves used to make herbal teas and infusions for medicinal purposes.
